Phase-23.NAME: Compact Mobile User Cabinet
Phase-23.goal: Redesign the user personal cabinet into a compact mobile-first workflow focused on VPN access, subscription state, device-bound configs, QR/download actions, and payment entry points.

scope.included: User dashboard layout, mobile navigation, device/config actions, subscription summary, payment entry points, and compact UI rules inside the user frontend.
scope.excluded: Backend API redesign, deployment changes, install scripts, billing provider changes, admin console implementation, and session-storage hardening.
scope.phase-boundary: Admin mobile console is Phase-24. Session security hardening is Phase-25. Existing deploy/install scripts remain untouched.

current-inventory.screen-layout: Desktop-first 320px sidebar, large status panel, large header copy, and repeated user/account cards. Mobile execution should replace this with a compact shell, concise identity/status line, and phone-safe navigation.
current-inventory.screen-dashboard: Current page uses a decorative hero, four metric cards, extra server cards, and a quick-action panel. Primary mobile content is VPN status, config action, subscription state, and active device summary; traffic and referral actions are secondary.
current-inventory.screen-config: Current page owns create/rotate/revoke, QR, copy, download, and raw config display. Mobile execution must turn this into a compact device/config workflow and avoid wide raw config overflow on small screens.
current-inventory.screen-subscription: Current page uses large status and plan cards. Mobile execution must keep active status, days left, plan price, duration, and payment button readable without oversized cards.
current-inventory.screen-secondary: Referral and settings surfaces stay available but must not displace VPN config, subscription, or device controls from the primary phone workflow.

data-flows.DF-UI-USER-HOME: Authenticated user opens the cabinet, frontend reads current user, VPN stats, user stats, subscription, and device list, then renders a compact home with VPN state, subscription state, config CTA, and device summary.
data-flows.DF-UI-CONFIG-ACTION: User taps primary config action, selects existing or newly created device when needed, then can open QR, download .conf, or copy config without visiting a wide desktop layout.
data-flows.DF-UI-DEVICE-MANAGEMENT: User views device slots, creates a named device, rotates an active device config, or revokes one device through compact rows and confirmation-safe controls.
data-flows.DF-UI-BILLING: User sees active/expired subscription status and reaches plan purchase or renewal from the mobile dashboard and subscription page without backend API changes.
data-flows.DF-UI-MOBILE-VERIFY: Phase verification checks 360x740, 390x844, and 430x932 viewports for no horizontal scroll, no overlapping text, and primary VPN config access within one or two taps.

Phase-23.step-1:
Convert the current inventory into an execution map: primary mobile surfaces are home, config/device, and subscription; secondary surfaces are referrals, settings, traffic detail, and raw config text.
Updated frontend-user module map if source ownership changes during execution. Documented primary/secondary/removable content priority in M-036 and M-038.
Phase-23.step-2:
Build the compact mobile home workflow with VPN status, primary config action, QR/download entry, subscription state, and active device summary.
Config action is visible on first screen or reachable after one navigation tap. Expired or missing subscription shows a payment CTA instead of dead config controls. Long email, device, and status text wraps or truncates safely without horizontal page scroll.
Phase-23.step-3:
Redesign subscription and payment surfaces into compact plan rows or concise cards with clear current status, expiry, price, duration, and renewal action.
Plan purchase still calls the existing billing API contract. Current subscription state remains visible above plan selection on phones.
Phase-23.step-4:
Redesign device-bound config management for create, rotate, revoke, QR, copy, and download without wide tables or raw config overflow.
Device rows fit phone width and keep destructive actions visually secondary. QR and .conf download remain available for the selected or newly created device. Raw config text is collapsed, wrapped, or placed behind a deliberate action on mobile.
Phase-23.step-5:
Apply compact UI system rules to the user frontend: no nested cards, no decorative hero/glass clutter, stable button/list sizing, concise navigation, and phone-safe text.
Cards and panels are used only where they frame repeated items, modals, or concrete tools. Buttons use existing lucide icons where available and have stable touch-friendly dimensions. Background styling does not rely on decorative radial gradients or oversized hero composition.
Phase-23.step-6:
Verify build and mobile smoke paths for login guard, dashboard, config QR/download/copy, subscription purchase entry, and device create/rotate/revoke controls. V-M-036,V-M-038
cd frontend && npm run build passes. Viewport smoke evidence records no horizontal document scroll at 360x740, 390x844, and 430x932. No backend API shape changes are introduced in Phase-23.

risk-controls.risk-1: Do not hide QR/download behind a desktop-only route or a low-priority menu.
risk-controls.risk-2: Do not mix Phase-25 token/localStorage hardening into this UI phase.
risk-controls.risk-3: Do not touch deploy, install, docker, nginx, or remote-server automation in this phase.
risk-controls.risk-4: Do not remove device-bound create/rotate/revoke flows while simplifying layout.

verification.check-2: Mobile viewport has no horizontal scroll or overlapping text at 360x740, 390x844, and 430x932.
verification.check-3: Primary VPN config action is reachable within one or two taps.
verification.check-4: QR, .conf download, copy, create device, rotate device, revoke device, subscription renewal, and payment entry remain present.
verification.check-5: Existing backend API contracts remain unchanged unless explicitly approved later.
